_comment: Per-chart narratives. CI (build_prompt.py -> GitHub Models -> write_narrative.py) overwrites these from live numbers; this committed copy is the fallback so the page always reads well. source = 'fallback' | 'github-models'.

notes.basket: The breakfast basket, an aggregate price index of coffee, sugar, wheat, and orange juice set to 100 in 2015, currently stands at 141, reflecting a roughly 41% increase over that baseline. This rise reflects recent and uneven gains across these commodities rather than a steady climb. The index captures global commodity prices compiled from FRED and IMF data. The overall increase signals notable cost escalation in everyday soft commodities used at breakfast.
notes.movers: Among the basket components, coffee has surged the most, nearly doubling since 2015 with a 99% increase, followed by orange juice advancing 34%, wheat rising 19%, and sugar up 13%. These price moves largely result from weather events and crop diseases—such as frost and drought affecting coffee arabica regions, and issues like citrus greening and hurricanes impacting orange juice production. These are relatively thin and volatile soft commodity markets, meaning localized disruptions can disproportionately affect global prices rather than broad inflationary trends.
notes.why: The headline rise in the breakfast basket is driven by a handful of weather-sensitive crops vulnerable to natural shocks. Given the nature of these agricultural risks and market thinness, elevated prices may persist for multiple years before normalizing. This underscores the sensitivity of some food commodity prices to environmental factors more than general economic inflation.
